name: Helium
summary: Day 79 of Hormuz blockade (Sunday). **HELIUM CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 NOW DAY 2 OF 14 — TrendForce 'two-week clock' counting down; ~12 days remain.** Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting) analyst take: tsunami framing held through weekend. **The Samsung 18-day strike confirmed May 21 to June 7 is a SEPARATE supply-side overlay that compounds with the helium clock.** Yesterday's brief flagged the partial pre-validation by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI move; the weekend held without new operational news, but Monday's KOSPI open will be the first test of whether Friday's selloff was Samsung-strike priced-in or Iran-preparation-not-yet-priced-in. **JPMorgan's ₩43 trillion ($28.8 billion) worst-case loss anchor remains the public benchmark.** KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric — and now additionally compound with the kinetic-preparation tail risk on Iran. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis lands; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. Samsung HeRS recycling system covers only ~18.6% of consumption. SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed PRODUCTION HALT yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **PRIOR (May 16):** HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16; per the original March 24 framework, ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates). **Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting) analyst take**: 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' framing held through Friday — but the helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY. **The Samsung 18-day strike May 21 is a SEPARATE-SUPPLY-SIDE OVERLAY that markets which don't distinguish helium-causation from labour-disruption will CONFLATE, producing exaggerated KOSPI/SMH moves regardless of helium-specific tightness.** JPMorgan ₩43T strike-loss scenario is materially worse than the $700M/day daily-rate framing yesterday's brief used. **The new graph trigger 'Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' is partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI**; next 5 sessions are now the live test window. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis lands; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric. **Kornbluth's structural helium-tightness call has been validated by the SK Hynix capacity-zero reporting and the unprecedented hyperscaler financing proposals.** Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers; KOSPI -6.1% Thursday is partial validation. PRIOR (May 15): **HELIUM COUNTDOWN: HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TOMORROW — KOREAN FAB BUFFER ~3 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 fully elapsed; Day 74 since Ras Laffan halt March 2).** **A9 SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY NOW 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting (Tom's Hardware, Wccftech, TradingKey, ad-hoc-news, Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg). Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (each running hundreds of millions of dollars). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at potentially discounted prices could crimp future profitability. Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **A9 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE PLANNED FROM MAY 21** after talks broke down May 12-13 (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times, Star, UPI). Union demands removing the existing bonus cap, allocating 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, and formalising terms in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike already caused an 18% drop in memory fab output and a 58% drop in foundry output on the affected night shift**, with full-stoppage projections at approximately 1 trillion won ($700M) per day. PM Kim Min-seok has called an emergency ministerial meeting. **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23, the Korean fab complex now faces three simultaneous supply-side stressors: helium scarcity, an 18-day strike from May 21, and zero spare capacity.** **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 (intraday roundtrip from 8,046.78 to 7,493) — single largest equity move of the cycle** is the price-discovery confirmation that the supply-side stack is no longer in 'no priced risk' mode. **NEW TRIGGER WATCHING**: 'Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI / SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' (markets dossier F3 proposed sub-trigger). **Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption**; Korea sources 64.7% from Qatar (2025). SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed PRODUCTION HALT yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers**; KOSPI -6.1% today is partial validation. Phil Kornbluth analyst take: 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session, but the operational picture compounded today: Samsung 18-day strike beginning May 21 *is* an effective production cut event regardless of helium causation, and could conflate with helium scarcity in markets that don't distinguish. **Falsification**: Samsung or SK Hynix supply guidance cut within 60 days lands the thesis; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 refutes it. — TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 fully elapsed (Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2); helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23 — MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN GRAPH**. Per markets dossier E proposed trigger: 'First Tier-1 fab announces helium-driven production cut' would cascade to kospi, taiwan-taiex, semiconductors, india-pharma (cryo-helium for MRI/labs). Kornbluth 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session. **C4 KOSPI +1.2% 1D / +36.7% 1M / +39.9% 3M continues AI super-cycle rally — pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is the most quantifiable real-world risk in the entire complex.** SK hynix says 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed production halt yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **Linde $513.26 (+1.9% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +6.8% 3M)** — industrial gases (helium proxy) bid INTACT today, suggesting industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage even as Korean fab buffer drains. SMH $572.46 (+2.0% 1D, +26.7% 1M, +40.5% 3M); KOSPI +1.2% 1D / +36.7% 1M; Taiwan TAIEX +0.9% 1D / +15.0% 1M / +23.6% 3M (Samsung+SK Hynix 42% of KOSPI; AI memory super-cycle; JPM raised KOSPI 12m bull-case target to 10,000). **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers**. 30% of global helium routed through Qatar Ras Laffan halted March 2; Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry). Cape rerouting working but doesn't replace volume. PRIOR (May 13): CYCLE'S HELIUM-TAIL TRADE THESIS HAS NOT BEEN CHALLENGED BY NEW DATA THIS SESSION.** **A13 SOUTH KOREAN GOVERNMENT FORMALLY CONFIRMED HMM NAMU MAY 4 EXTERNAL STRIKE (double-tap drone pattern); FM Cho Hyun 'too early' on attribution + PPP opposition pressure = SEOUL INSTITUTIONALLY MAINTAINING 'VARIOUS ACTORS' HEDGE.** KOSPI +2.8% rebound after May 12 -1.7% (FM Cho hedge defuses immediate diplomatic friction). USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D) disconnect = capital outflow despite equity relief. Korean fab buffer 4 weeks remaining; Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification HOLDS but window contracting. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce March 24 'two-week clock' now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. SMH $561.25 (-2.6% 1D on profit-take after AI vertical run, +26.6% 1M, +37.7% 3M); rotation from AI-megacap to industrial-cyclical (Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6%); Linde -0.1% 1D (+2.3% 1M); BASF -0.5%; industrial-gas rotation FAILED (chip customers on long-term contracts not spot). **Phil Kornbluth (analyst takes): 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session.** Watch: Samsung or SK Hynix supply guidance cut within 60 days lands the thesis. Spot helium retracing to pre-war levels by July 1 refutes it. Counter-claim: SK hynix's 'diversified supplies' is one-sided (CLAIMED); TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED; no confirmed production halt yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **KOREAN FAB BUFFER NOW ~4 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (down from ~5 weeks May 10).** Tier-1 chipmaker falsification still HOLDS: no Samsung or SK Hynix production halt yet — but **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran**, creating diplomatic friction with Trump and exposing the Korea-supply-chain vulnerability narratively. SK hynix says it has 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce's March-24 'two-week clock' now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt — original Discovery Alert thesis stretched but not falsified. Spot ultra-pure helium up 40-100% / contract +40% (gasworld + J2 Sourcing); ultra-pure ~doubled. **SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M, +41.9% 3M) — AI-INFRASTRUCTURE-CAPEX DEMAND CONTINUES TO EXCEED HELIUM-CONSTRAINT IMPACT; Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' framework intact.** Linde $504.40 (+2.3% 1D) — industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage; Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. **KOSPI -1.7% 1D BUT +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M — first profit-taking session after extraordinary vertical run; Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor' with HMM Namu forensics.** USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on Korea-Iran-attribution friction with Trump. Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting): 'There is a tsunami coming, but it's still a thousand miles offshore. Right now, it's still sunny on the beach.' Estimates net global shortage at ~15% (30% Qatari capacity loss offset by prior 15% supply overhang); spot helium up 40-100% in some channels; falsification: Samsung/SK Hynix production-halt within 60 days. **THE COUNTDOWN MAKES THIS THE MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN THE GRAPH — will EITHER activate OR be disproven within ~4 weeks.** Qatar 30-38% global supply still offline; Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry). Cape rerouting working.

recent_signals.headline: **HELIUM COUNTDOWN: Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2 — TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 fully elapsed (8 weeks); Korean fab buffer ~3 weeks remaining; HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 — MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN GRAPH.** Per markets dossier C4 + E: KOSPI is most exposed major index (Samsung+SK Hynix 42%); AI super-cycle (KOSPI +1.2% 1D / +36.7% 1M / +39.9% 3M; SMH $572.46 +2.0% 1D / +26.7% 1M / +40.5% 3M; Taiwan TAIEX +0.9% / +15.0% 1M) pricing ZERO supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. **Linde $513.26 +1.9% 1D / +2.7% 1M = industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage** — bid intact despite chip customers on long-term contracts. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED, one-sided); no Samsung/SK hynix production-halt or production-cut announcement. Per markets dossier H reversibility map: KOSPI -15-25% / SMH -20-30% snap-back if helium-shutdown materialises. **New trigger added: First Tier-1 fab announces helium-driven production cut** (less stringent than full halt — confirmed production cut from fab itself, not analyst inference) — would cascade to KOSPI/Taiwan/semiconductors/india-pharma (cryo-helium for MRI/labs).

recent_signals.headline: HELIUM CHOKEPOINT BUFFER CLOCK NOW ~5 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (down from ~5-6 weeks yesterday). SUNDAY BULK-CARRIER-NEAR-DOHA STRIKE OPERATIONALLY ADJACENT TO RAS LAFFAN — Qatar's main LNG/helium export complex. Was strike location coincidental or deliberate? If deliberate, signals graduated escalation against Qatari helium/LNG export infrastructure rather than against US-blockade-facilitating shipping. Markets remain CLOSED Sunday — Friday May 8 still operative: SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M, +39.3% 3M); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+0.1% 1D, +36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M); SK Hynix +144% YTD; Samsung +111% YTD = AI cycle dominating helium tail-risk in markets. Cheniere CCO Q1 May 7: 'astounded that gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month' — generalized industrial-gas comment markets have absorbed supply shock without demand destruction so far. Korean fabs source 65% helium from Qatar. The most TIME-SENSITIVE trigger in graph: trigger 'Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage' will EITHER activate OR be disproven within ~5 weeks. SK hynix says 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce 'two-week clock' analysis now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. Linde flat May 8 ($493.16, -0.1% 1D), BASF +1.5% (€51.65) — industrial-gas-producer rotation thesis FAILED.
sources: UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
Phil Kornbluth / Smith Web (Korean fab buffers)

historical_summaries.summary: 6 signals. Iran war threatening supply of helium -- 30% of world supply from Qatar's Ras Laffan, now offline | Analysts project minimum 2-3 month helium production shutdown, 4-6 months before supply chain returns | TrendForce published analysis naming South Korea as hardest-hit -- Samsung and SK Hynix sourced 64

22 signals. NOW IN TrendForce's predicted early-April exhaustion window | CONFIRMED: Helium spot prices have doubled from pre-crisis levels; 30-38% of global supply offline due to Ras Laffan force majeure | CONFIRMED: KOSPI surged +8
